 {"id":"bd-4e21b5ad","title":"Add test case for symmetric collision (both clones create same ID simultaneously)","description":"TestTwoCloneCollision demonstrates the problem, but we need a simpler unit test for the collision resolver itself.

Test should verify:
- Two issues with same ID, different content
- Content hash determines winner deterministically  
- Result is same regardless of which clone imports first
- No title swapping occurs

This can be a simpler test than the full integration test.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-10-29T17:46:10.046999-07:00","updated_at":"2025-10-31T12:00:43.196705-07:00","closed_at":"2025-10-31T12:00:43.196705-07:00"}
 {"id":"bd-4ec8","title":"Widespread double JSON encoding bug in daemon mode RPC calls","description":"Multiple CLI commands had the same double JSON encoding bug found in bd-1048. All commands that called ResolveID via RPC used string(resp.Data) instead of properly unmarshaling the JSON response. This caused IDs to retain JSON quotes ("bd-1048" instead of bd-1048), which then got double-encoded when passed to subsequent RPC calls.

Affected commands:
- bd show (3 instances)
- bd dep add/remove/tree (5 instances)
- bd label add/remove/list (3 instances)
- bd reopen (1 instance)

Root cause: resp.Data is json.RawMessage (already JSON-encoded), so string() conversion preserves quotes.

Fix: Replace all string(resp.Data) with json.Unmarshal(resp.Data, &id) for proper deserialization.

All commands now tested and working correctly with daemon mode.","status":"tombstone","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-11-02T22:33:01.632691-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-25T01:21:01.952723-08:00","deleted_at":"2025-12-25T01:21:01.952723-08:00","deleted_by":"batch delete","delete_reason":"batch delete","original_type":"bug"}
 {"id":"bd-4ew","title":"bd doctor should detect fresh clone and recommend 'bd init'","description":"When running `bd doctor` on a fresh clone (JSONL exists, no .db file), it should:

1. Detect this is a fresh clone situation
2. Recommend `bd init --prefix <detected-prefix>` as the fix
3. Show the prefix detected from the JSONL file

Currently it shows various warnings (git hooks, merge driver, etc.) but doesn't address the fundamental issue: the database needs to be hydrated.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-11-27T20:21:15.691764-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-02T17:11:19.731628399-05:00","closed_at":"2025-11-28T22:14:49.092112-08:00"}
-{"id":"bd-4f43s","title":"PR Sheriff Standing Orders","description":"## Role: PR Sheriff (Emma)

Review incoming PRs on steveyegge/beads and farm out "easy wins" to crew workers.

## Easy-Win Criteria (NO human discretion needed)
- Bug fixes with clear reproduction and solution
- Documentation fixes (typos, broken links, clarifications)
- Simple feature additions that align with existing patterns
- Shell script fixes (portability, edge cases)
- Test additions for existing functionality

## NOT Easy-Wins (leave for human to review)
- Security-related changes
- Architectural decisions
- API changes
- Anything controversial or requiring product judgment
- PRs that touch core functionality in non-obvious ways

## PR States to Skip
- **Changes requested** - Already reviewed, waiting on contributor. Skip.
- **Draft PRs** - Not ready for review. Skip.

## Workflow
1. Run: gh pr list --repo steveyegge/beads --state open
2. Skip PRs with "Changes requested" review status
3. For remaining PRs, review with: gh pr view <num> --repo steveyegge/beads
4. Categorize: easy-win, needs-human, or skip (spam/stale)
5. For easy-wins: create bead, sling to available crew worker
6. Track assignments in beads
7. NO need to send reports to mayor - human will review PRs on their own schedule

## Available Crew
- dave, fang, giles, grip, wolf

## Commands
- gh pr view <num> --repo steveyegge/beads
- gh pr diff <num> --repo steveyegge/beads
- gt sling <bead-id> beads/crew/<name> -m "context"","status":"hooked","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"beads/crew/emma","created_at":"2026-01-08T14:16:52.562453-08:00","created_by":"beads/crew/emma","updated_at":"2026-01-12T01:26:30.697175-08:00"}
+{"id":"bd-4f43s","title":"PR Sheriff Standing Orders","description":"## Role: PR Sheriff (Emma)

Review incoming PRs on steveyegge/beads and farm out "easy wins" to crew workers.

## Easy-Win Criteria (NO human discretion needed)
- Bug fixes with clear reproduction and solution
- Documentation fixes (typos, broken links, clarifications)
- Simple feature additions that align with existing patterns
- Shell script fixes (portability, edge cases)
- Test additions for existing functionality

## NOT Easy-Wins (leave for human to review)
- Security-related changes
- Architectural decisions
- API changes
- Anything controversial or requiring product judgment
- PRs that touch core functionality in non-obvious ways

## PR States to Skip
- **Changes requested** - Already reviewed, waiting on contributor. Skip.
- **Draft PRs** - Not ready for review. Skip.

## Workflow
1. Run: gh pr list --repo steveyegge/beads --state open --search "review:none review:approved -review:changes_requested"
2. For each PR, review with: gh pr view <num> --repo steveyegge/beads
3. Categorize: easy-win, needs-human, or skip (spam/stale)
4. For easy-wins: create bead, sling to available crew worker
5. Track assignments in beads
6. NO need to send reports to mayor - human will review PRs on their own schedule

## Available Crew
- dave, fang, giles, grip, wolf

## Commands
- gh pr list --repo steveyegge/beads --state open --search "review:none review:approved -review:changes_requested"
- gh pr view <num> --repo steveyegge/beads
- gh pr diff <num> --repo steveyegge/beads
- gt sling <bead-id> beads/crew/<name> -m "context"","status":"hooked","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"beads/crew/emma","created_at":"2026-01-08T14:16:52.562453-08:00","created_by":"beads/crew/emma","updated_at":"2026-01-12T01:40:16.42156-08:00"}

 {"id":"bd-be7a","title":"Create npm package structure with package.json","description":"Set up initial npm package structure for @beads/bd:

## Files to create
- npm/package.json - Package metadata, dependencies, scripts
- npm/bin/bd - CLI wrapper script that invokes native binary
- npm/.gitignore - Ignore downloaded binaries
- npm/README.md - Installation and usage instructions

## package.json structure
- Name: @beads/bd (scoped package)
- Main: index.js (exports binary path)
- Bin: bin/bd (CLI entry point)
- Scripts: postinstall (download binary)
- Keywords: issue-tracker, cli, beads, bd
- License: MIT

## Bin wrapper
Simple Node.js script that:
- Spawns native binary with child_process.spawn
- Passes through all arguments and stdio
- Exits with binary's exit code","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-11-02T23:39:47.416779-08:00","updated_at":"2025-11-03T10:31:45.381258-08:00","closed_at":"2025-11-03T10:31:45.381258-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-be7a","depends_on_id":"bd-febc","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-11-02T23:40:32.923859-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
